examples.title: Basic sigsetjmp/siglongjmp Example
examples.description: Demonstrates basic usage of sigsetjmp and siglongjmp for error recovery
examples.explanation: This example shows: sigsetjmp with savemask=1 saves signal mask, returns 0 initially. When signal handler calls siglongjmp with value 1, sigsetjmp returns 1, allowing recovery code to execute. The volatile sig_atomic_t ensures the flag is handled correctly.

examples.title: Error Recovery Pattern
examples.description: Shows a practical error recovery pattern using sigsetjmp/siglongjmp
examples.explanation: This demonstrates a try/catch-like pattern using sigsetjmp/siglongjmp. sigsetjmp returns 0 on first call, then returns the value (99) passed to siglongjmp when jumped back. This allows structured error recovery without exceptions.

examples.title: Comparison: savemask=0 vs savemask=1
examples.description: Demonstrates the difference between saving and not saving signal mask
examples.explanation: When savemask=0, siglongjmp doesn't restore any signal mask (behaves like longjmp). When savemask=1, the signal mask at the time of sigsetjmp is restored by siglongjmp. This is critical for proper signal handling.

examples.title: Volatile Variable Usage
examples.description: Shows why volatile is important with siglongjmp
examples.explanation: The compiler may optimize 'counter' since it appears unchanged after the siglongjmp call site. volatile forces the compiler to read/write the variable each time, ensuring correct behavior after the jump.
